textPlain: > In 2023, hackers used vishing (voice phishing) to impersonate employees and gain access to the internal systems of MGM Resorts International and Caesars Entertainment on the Las Vegas Strip, causing hundreds of millions of dollars in financial losses.First time I’ve heard the term “vishing” to describe the attack we’ve all seen coming.
If a hastily organized band of teenagers can pull this off, you have to wonder what APTs are capable of.
I’m almost positive ripping off a casino isn’t a crime. I’d be demanding a jury trial for sure.

Phishing (Email), Smishing (SMS/Text Messages), and Vishing (Voice) are all standard industry terms, though obviously phishing is most well known.Then there's even subcategories that further define some of these, like Spear Phishing, Whaling.The industry loves its fun naming.
social engineering is as old as hacking itself
Legal: using your brain. Illegal: devices, collusion, past-posting, edge-sorting with marked cards. Juries know the difference.
